mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or the signal shows zero predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of instances when both thresholds are breached, or the joint condition fails to separate market regimes in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows despite consistent payroll growth, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent signals severe economic contraction and declining corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of quarters where GDP growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to predict downturns in historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 52%, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for monetary policy shocks.
